name: Tofu"turkey" with Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. Mash the tofu or mix well with hands, ensuring all lumps are out.
2. Line a 12-inch colander with wet cheesecloth, overlapping sides.
3. Add mashed tofu to the cloth-covered colander and press down. Cover with the overlapping sides.
4. Place the whole setup in a large bowl. Cover the cheesecloth with a plate that fits inside the colander and place a 5-pound weight on it. Refrigerate for 2 to 3 hours.
5. When ready, hollow out the tofu to within 1 inch of the sides and bottom. Place the tofu in a bowl.
6. Stuff the tofu with the stuffing mixture and pack firmly. Cover with remaining tofu and pat down.
7. Turn stuffed tofu onto a greased baking sheet, flat side down.
8. Gently press on the sides of "turkey" to achieve a more oval shape. Mold "drumsticks" out of one pound of tofu and place on each side if desired.
9. Mix basting mixture and brush half onto the tofu turkey. Cover with foil and bake at 400°F for about 1 hour.
10. Remove foil, baste with remaining mixture (except a few tablespoons), and return to oven for 1 more hour or until golden brown.
11. Remove from oven and serve with gravy of choice and cranberry sauce if desired. Leftover tastes great in sandwiches or plain.
